qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path Traversal in Plugin Public File Serving Endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes a path traversal vulnerability (CWE-22) in the plugin public file serving endpoint (/public/:plugin/*filepath). The advisory describes an authorization bypass (CWE-863) where tenant admins can create tenants on the root domain due to role context mismatch. These are different vulnerability classes affecting different components with different root causes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes an authorization bypass where tenant admins can create tenants on the root domain using their tenant-admin role context (CWE-863). The only qualifying finding describes a path traversal vulnerability in the plugin public file serving endpoint (CWE-22). These are different vulnerability classes, different affected components (tenant creation logic vs. static file serving), and different root causes (role context mismatch vs. incomplete path sanitization).
evidence: Advisory: Tenant role context mismatch allows unauthorized tenant creation on root domain (CWE-863)
Advisory: Affects tenant creation endpoint with role_id check logic
Finding: Affects static file serving with incomplete path sanitization
Different vulnerable components, different attack vectors, different root causes
